13: 
14: # Complexity Decision Matrix - Quick Reference for Level Selection
15: 
16: Quick reference for complexity-based level selection and feature enabling.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: ### Purpose
23: 
24: This matrix provides quick-reference guidance for selecting the appropriate documentation level based on task complexity. Use it to determine which templates to use and which features to enable.
25: 
26: For cross-phase campaigns, treat boundary-management overhead as part of the complexity score. Tasks that span multiple spec folders or runtime families should be scored as coordination-heavy even when each individual edit is small.
27: 
28: ### Usage
29: 
30: 1. Estimate your task's complexity score using the dimension signals in Section 3
31: 2. Match the score to a level in Section 2
32: 3. Verify feature requirements in Section 4
33: 4. Use the CLI commands in Section 8 to create your spec folder
34: 5. If the work crosses phases or multiple runtime ownership boundaries, add coordination weight before choosing a level
35: 
36: ---
37: 
38: ## 2. LEVEL SELECTION BY SCORE
39: 
40: | Score Range | Level | Name         | Templates                                   | Features Auto-Enabled         |
41: | ----------- | ----- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
42: | 0-25        | 1     | Baseline     | spec, plan, tasks, impl-summary             | None                          |
43: | 26-55       | 2     | Verification | Level 1 + checklist                         | dep-graph (table)             |
44: | 56-79       | 3     | Full         | Level 2 + decision-record                   | dep-graph (ASCII), effort-est |
45: | 80-100      | 3+    | Extended     | Level 3 + AI protocols + extended checklist | All features                  |
46: 
47: **Coordination-Root Override**: Root coordination packets governing multi-phase efforts
48: (phase maps, ADR records, tree-truth governance) should be classified as Level 3+
49: regardless of complexity score, as they require governance artifacts not covered by Level 3.
50: 
51: ---
52: 
53: ## 3. DIMENSION SCORING SIGNALS
54: 
55: ### Scope (25% weight)
56: 
57: | Signal             | Points | Example                    |
58: | ------------------ | ------ | -------------------------- |
59: | 100+ LOC mentioned | +15    | "about 200 lines"          |
60: | 500+ LOC mentioned | +25    | "major rewrite"            |
61: | 3-5 files affected | +10    | "update three files"       |
62: | 6+ files affected  | +20    | "affects multiple modules" |
63: | Multiple systems   | +15    | "frontend and backend"     |
64: | "comprehensive"    | +10    | "comprehensive redesign"   |
65: | "large-scale"      | +15    | "large-scale migration"    |
66: 
67: ### Risk (25% weight)
68: 
69: | Signal           | Points | Example                                 |
70: | ---------------- | ------ | --------------------------------------- |
71: | Authentication   | +20    | "login", "auth", "session"              |
72: | Authorization    | +15    | "permissions", "access control"         |
73: | API changes      | +12    | "endpoint", "REST", "GraphQL"           |
74: | Database         | +15    | "migration", "schema", "query"          |
75: | Security         | +20    | "security", "encryption", "credentials" |
76: | Configuration    | +10    | "config", "environment", "settings"     |
77: | Breaking changes | +15    | "breaking", "deprecate", "remove API"   |
78: 
79: ### Research (20% weight)
80: 
81: | Signal        | Points | Example                             |
82: | ------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------- |
83: | "investigate" | +15    | "investigate the issue"             |
84: | "analyze"     | +12    | "analyze performance"               |
85: | "research"    | +15    | "research alternatives"             |
86: | "explore"     | +10    | "explore options"                   |
87: | "unknown"     | +15    | "behavior is unknown"               |
88: | External deps | +12    | "third-party", "library", "service" |
89: 
90: ### Multi-Agent (15% weight)
91: 
92: | Signal          | Points | Example                       |
93: | --------------- | ------ | ----------------------------- |
94: | "parallel"      | +20    | "parallel workstreams"        |
95: | "concurrent"    | +15    | "concurrent tasks"            |
96: | Multiple agents | +25    | "5 agents", "multiple agents" |
97: | Workstreams     | +20    | "separate workstreams"        |
98: | Coordination    | +15    | "coordinate between"          |
99: 
100: ### Coordination (15% weight)
101: 
102: | Signal                 | Points | Example                               |
103: | ---------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------- |
104: | Dependencies           | +15    | "depends on", "blocking"              |
105: | Cross-system           | +20    | "across systems", "integration"       |
106: | External services      | +15    | "external API", "third-party service" |
107: | Blocking relationships | +15    | "must complete before"                |
108: | Complex ordering       | +10    | "specific order", "sequence"          |
109: 
110: ---
111: 
112: ## 4. FEATURE DECISION MATRIX
113: 
114: | Feature               | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3  | Level 3+ |
115: | --------------------- | ------- | ------- | -------- | -------- |
116: | Basic templates       | ✓       | ✓       | ✓        | ✓        |
117: | Checklist             | -       | ✓       | ✓        | ✓        |
118: | Decision record       | -       | -       | ✓        | ✓        |
119: | Dep graph (table)     | -       | ✓       | ✓        | ✓        |
120: | Dep graph (ASCII)     | -       | -       | ✓        | ✓        |
121: | Dep graph (DAG)       | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
122: | Effort estimation     | -       | -       | ✓        | ✓        |
123: | AI execution protocol | -       | -       | Optional | Required |
124: | Extended checklist    | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
125: | Approval tracking     | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
126: | Workstream org        | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
127: 
128: ---
129: 
130: ## 5. CONTENT SCALING MATRIX
131: 
132: | Content Type         | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3 | Level 3+ |
133: | -------------------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | -------- |
134: | User Stories         | 1-2     | 2-4     | 4-8     | 8-15     |
135: | Phases               | 2-3     | 3-5     | 5-8     | 8-12     |
136: | Tasks                | 5-15    | 15-50   | 50-100  | 100+     |
137: | Checklist items      | 10-20   | 30-50   | 60-100  | 100-150  |
138: | Requirements         | 3-5     | 5-10    | 8-15    | 15+      |
139: | Acceptance scenarios | 2-4     | 4-8     | 6-12    | 12+      |
140: 
141: ---
142: 
143: ## 6. QUICK DECISION GUIDE
144: 
145: ### When to Override Auto-Detection
146: 
147: **Override to HIGHER level when:**
148: - Security-sensitive changes (even if low complexity score)
149: - Breaking API changes
150: - Database migrations
151: - Multi-team coordination required
152: - Compliance/audit requirements
153: 
154: **Override to LOWER level when:**
155: - False positive on keywords ("research" used casually)
156: - Simple task described with complex language
157: - One-time utility script
158: 
159: ### Template Style Selection
160: 
161: Choose the appropriate template **style** based on user experience and requirement clarity:
162: 
163: | Style    | Lines  | Best For                              | Path                 |
164: | -------- | ------ | ------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
165: | **Core** | ~60-90 | Experienced users, clear requirements | manifest-backed Level contract |
166: 
167: ### Confidence Thresholds
168: 
169: | Confidence | Action                              |
170: | ---------- | ----------------------------------- |
171: | 85-95%     | Trust recommendation                |
172: | 70-84%     | Review dimensions, may accept       |
173: | 50-69%     | Review carefully, consider override |
174: | <50%       | Manual selection recommended        |
175: 
176: ### Boundary Cases
177: 
178: | Score | Near Boundary?  | Recommendation                                    |
179: | ----- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
180: | 23-27 | L1/L2 boundary  | If any risk factors, choose L2                    |
181: | 53-57 | L2/L3 boundary  | If architectural impact, choose L3                |
182: | 77-82 | L3/L3+ boundary | If multi-agent or AI protocols needed, choose L3+ |
183: 
184: ---
185: 
186: ## 7. EXAMPLE CLASSIFICATIONS
187: 
188: | Task Description                         | Score | Level | Key Factors                      |
189: | ---------------------------------------- | ----- | ----- | -------------------------------- |
190: | "Fix typo in README"                     | 8     | 1     | Low scope, no risk               |
191: | "Add email validation to form"           | 22    | 1     | Single file, clear scope         |
192: | "Create reusable modal component"        | 38    | 2     | Multiple files, needs testing    |
193: | "Implement OAuth2 authentication"        | 62    | 3     | Auth risk, multiple systems      |
194: | "Add payment processing with Stripe"     | 71    | 3     | High risk, external integration  |
195: | "Platform migration with 10 workstreams" | 89    | 3+    | Multi-agent, high coordination   |
196: | "Real-time collaboration feature"        | 85    | 3+    | Complex state, high coordination |
197: 
198: ---
199: 
200: ## 8. CLI QUICK REFERENCE
201: 
202: ```bash
203: # Create spec folder with Level 1 (default)
204: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Simple bugfix"
205: 
206: # Create spec folder with Level 2
207: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Add authentication" --level 2
208: 
209: # Create spec folder with Level 3
210: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Major refactor" --level 3
211: 
212: # Create spec folder with Level 3+ (extended)
213: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Platform migration" --level 3+
214: ```
215: 
216: ---
217: 
218: ## 9. SPEC TRACEABILITY
219: 
220: This decision matrix addresses the following spec requirements:
221: 
222: | Requirement | Section | Description                                       |
223: | ----------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
224: | REQ-011     | §4.1    | Complexity scoring algorithm and dimensions       |
225: | REQ-012     | §4.2    | Score-to-level mapping (0-25, 26-55, 56-79, 80+)  |
226: | REQ-013     | §4.3    | Dimension signals and keyword detection           |
227: | REQ-014     | §4.4    | Feature enablement based on complexity            |
228: | REQ-015     | §4.5    | Override conditions for manual level selection    |
229: 
230: **Source**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/spec.md`
231: 
232: ---
233: 
234: ## 10. RELATED RESOURCES
235: 
236: ### Asset Files
237: - [level_decision_matrix.md](./level_decision_matrix.md) - LOC-based level selection and progressive enhancement
238: - [template_mapping.md](./template_mapping.md) - Template routing with copy commands
239: - [parallel_dispatch_config.md](./parallel_dispatch_config.md) - Complexity scoring and agent dispatch
240: 
241: ### Reference Files
242: - level specifications reference - Complete Level 1-3+ requirements
243: - [template_guide.md](../references/templates/template_guide.md) - Template selection and quality standards
244: 
245: ### Scripts
246: - Level contract resolver - manifest-backed Level contract rendering
247: 
248: ### Related Skills
249: - `system-spec-kit` - Spec folder workflow orchestrator

(End of file - total 249 lines)

# Complexity Decision Matrix - Quick Reference for Level Selection

Quick reference for complexity-based level selection and feature enabling.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

This matrix provides quick-reference guidance for selecting the appropriate documentation level based on task complexity. Use it to determine which templates to use and which features to enable.

For cross-phase campaigns, treat boundary-management overhead as part of the complexity score. Tasks that span multiple spec folders or runtime families should be scored as coordination-heavy even when each individual edit is small.

### Usage

1. Estimate your task's complexity score using the dimension signals in Section 3
2. Match the score to a level in Section 2
3. Verify feature requirements in Section 4
4. Use the CLI commands in Section 8 to create your spec folder
5. If the work crosses phases or multiple runtime ownership boundaries, add coordination weight before choosing a level

---

## 2. LEVEL SELECTION BY SCORE

| Score Range | Level | Name         | Templates                                   | Features Auto-Enabled         |
| ----------- | ----- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| 0-25        | 1     | Baseline     | spec, plan, tasks, impl-summary             | None                          |
| 26-55       | 2     | Verification | Level 1 + checklist                         | dep-graph (table)             |
| 56-79       | 3     | Full         | Level 2 + decision-record                   | dep-graph (ASCII), effort-est |
| 80-100      | 3+    | Extended     | Level 3 + AI protocols + extended checklist | All features                  |

**Coordination-Root Override**: Root coordination packets governing multi-phase efforts
(phase maps, ADR records, tree-truth governance) should be classified as Level 3+
regardless of complexity score, as they require governance artifacts not covered by Level 3.

---

## 3. DIMENSION SCORING SIGNALS

### Scope (25% weight)

| Signal             | Points | Example                    |
| ------------------ | ------ | -------------------------- |
| 100+ LOC mentioned | +15    | "about 200 lines"          |
| 500+ LOC mentioned | +25    | "major rewrite"            |
| 3-5 files affected | +10    | "update three files"       |
| 6+ files affected  | +20    | "affects multiple modules" |
| Multiple systems   | +15    | "frontend and backend"     |
| "comprehensive"    | +10    | "comprehensive redesign"   |
| "large-scale"      | +15    | "large-scale migration"    |

### Risk (25% weight)

| Signal           | Points | Example                                 |
| ---------------- | ------ | --------------------------------------- |
| Authentication   | +20    | "login", "auth", "session"              |
| Authorization    | +15    | "permissions", "access control"         |
| API changes      | +12    | "endpoint", "REST", "GraphQL"           |
| Database         | +15    | "migration", "schema", "query"          |
| Security         | +20    | "security", "encryption", "credentials" |
| Configuration    | +10    | "config", "environment", "settings"     |
| Breaking changes | +15    | "breaking", "deprecate", "remove API"   |

### Research (20% weight)

| Signal        | Points | Example                             |
| ------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------- |
| "investigate" | +15    | "investigate the issue"             |
| "analyze"     | +12    | "analyze performance"               |
| "research"    | +15    | "research alternatives"             |
| "explore"     | +10    | "explore options"                   |
| "unknown"     | +15    | "behavior is unknown"               |
| External deps | +12    | "third-party", "library", "service" |

### Multi-Agent (15% weight)

| Signal          | Points | Example                       |
| --------------- | ------ | ----------------------------- |
| "parallel"      | +20    | "parallel workstreams"        |
| "concurrent"    | +15    | "concurrent tasks"            |
| Multiple agents | +25    | "5 agents", "multiple agents" |
| Workstreams     | +20    | "separate workstreams"        |
| Coordination    | +15    | "coordinate between"          |

### Coordination (15% weight)

| Signal                 | Points | Example                               |
| ---------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------- |
| Dependencies           | +15    | "depends on", "blocking"              |
| Cross-system           | +20    | "across systems", "integration"       |
| External services      | +15    | "external API", "third-party service" |
| Blocking relationships | +15    | "must complete before"                |
| Complex ordering       | +10    | "specific order", "sequence"          |

---

## 4. FEATURE DECISION MATRIX

| Feature               | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3  | Level 3+ |
| --------------------- | ------- | ------- | -------- | -------- |
| Basic templates       | ✓       | ✓       | ✓        | ✓        |
| Checklist             | -       | ✓       | ✓        | ✓        |
| Decision record       | -       | -       | ✓        | ✓        |
| Dep graph (table)     | -       | ✓       | ✓        | ✓        |
| Dep graph (ASCII)     | -       | -       | ✓        | ✓        |
| Dep graph (DAG)       | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
| Effort estimation     | -       | -       | ✓        | ✓        |
| AI execution protocol | -       | -       | Optional | Required |
| Extended checklist    | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
| Approval tracking     | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |
| Workstream org        | -       | -       | -        | ✓        |

---

## 5. CONTENT SCALING MATRIX

| Content Type         | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3 | Level 3+ |
| -------------------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | -------- |
| User Stories         | 1-2     | 2-4     | 4-8     | 8-15     |
| Phases               | 2-3     | 3-5     | 5-8     | 8-12     |
| Tasks                | 5-15    | 15-50   | 50-100  | 100+     |
| Checklist items      | 10-20   | 30-50   | 60-100  | 100-150  |
| Requirements         | 3-5     | 5-10    | 8-15    | 15+      |
| Acceptance scenarios | 2-4     | 4-8     | 6-12    | 12+      |

---

## 6. QUICK DECISION GUIDE

### When to Override Auto-Detection

**Override to HIGHER level when:**
- Security-sensitive changes (even if low complexity score)
- Breaking API changes
- Database migrations
- Multi-team coordination required
- Compliance/audit requirements

**Override to LOWER level when:**
- False positive on keywords ("research" used casually)
- Simple task described with complex language
- One-time utility script

### Template Style Selection

Choose the appropriate template **style** based on user experience and requirement clarity:

| Style    | Lines  | Best For                              | Path                 |
| -------- | ------ | ------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
| **Core** | ~60-90 | Experienced users, clear requirements | manifest-backed Level contract |

### Confidence Thresholds

| Confidence | Action                              |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------- |
| 85-95%     | Trust recommendation                |
| 70-84%     | Review dimensions, may accept       |
| 50-69%     | Review carefully, consider override |
| <50%       | Manual selection recommended        |

### Boundary Cases

| Score | Near Boundary?  | Recommendation                                    |
| ----- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| 23-27 | L1/L2 boundary  | If any risk factors, choose L2                    |
| 53-57 | L2/L3 boundary  | If architectural impact, choose L3                |
| 77-82 | L3/L3+ boundary | If multi-agent or AI protocols needed, choose L3+ |

---

## 7. EXAMPLE CLASSIFICATIONS

| Task Description                         | Score | Level | Key Factors                      |
| ---------------------------------------- | ----- | ----- | -------------------------------- |
| "Fix typo in README"                     | 8     | 1     | Low scope, no risk               |
| "Add email validation to form"           | 22    | 1     | Single file, clear scope         |
| "Create reusable modal component"        | 38    | 2     | Multiple files, needs testing    |
| "Implement OAuth2 authentication"        | 62    | 3     | Auth risk, multiple systems      |
| "Add payment processing with Stripe"     | 71    | 3     | High risk, external integration  |
| "Platform migration with 10 workstreams" | 89    | 3+    | Multi-agent, high coordination   |
| "Real-time collaboration feature"        | 85    | 3+    | Complex state, high coordination |

---

## 8. CLI QUICK REFERENCE

```bash
# Create spec folder with Level 1 (default)
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Simple bugfix"

# Create spec folder with Level 2
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Add authentication" --level 2

# Create spec folder with Level 3
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Major refactor" --level 3

# Create spec folder with Level 3+ (extended)
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh "Platform migration" --level 3+
```

---

## 9. SPEC TRACEABILITY

This decision matrix addresses the following spec requirements:

| Requirement | Section | Description                                       |
| ----------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| REQ-011     | §4.1    | Complexity scoring algorithm and dimensions       |
| REQ-012     | §4.2    | Score-to-level mapping (0-25, 26-55, 56-79, 80+)  |
| REQ-013     | §4.3    | Dimension signals and keyword detection           |
| REQ-014     | §4.4    | Feature enablement based on complexity            |
| REQ-015     | §4.5    | Override conditions for manual level selection    |

**Source**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/spec.md`

---

## 10. RELATED RESOURCES

### Asset Files
- [level_decision_matrix.md](./level_decision_matrix.md) - LOC-based level selection and progressive enhancement
- [template_mapping.md](./template_mapping.md) - Template routing with copy commands
- [parallel_dispatch_config.md](./parallel_dispatch_config.md) - Complexity scoring and agent dispatch

### Reference Files
- level specifications reference - Complete Level 1-3+ requirements
- [template_guide.md](../references/templates/template_guide.md) - Template selection and quality standards

### Scripts
- Level contract resolver - manifest-backed Level contract rendering

### Related Skills

1: ---
2: title: Level Decision Matrix
3: description: Quick reference for selecting the appropriate documentation level using progressive enhancement.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "level decision matrix"
6:   - "documentation level selection"
7:   - "loc soft guidance"
8:   - "secondary factors override"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: planning
11: version: 3.6.0.33
12: ---
13: 
14: # Level Decision Matrix - Complexity Scoring Guide
15: 
16: Quick reference for selecting documentation levels (1-3+) based on LOC guidance and complexity factors.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: ### Purpose
23: 
24: This matrix helps determine the appropriate documentation level for any task. Use it when:
25: - Starting a new spec folder and need to choose Level 1, 2, or 3
26: - Scope changes mid-implementation and level escalation may be needed
27: - Uncertain whether complexity/risk factors should override LOC guidance
28: - Coordinating a campaign that spans multiple phase folders or runtime families
29: 
30: ### Usage
31: 
32: 1. Check LOC estimate against thresholds (<100, 100-499, ≥500)
33: 2. Evaluate secondary factors (complexity, risk, dependencies, testing needs)
34: 3. If factors suggest higher level than LOC indicates, escalate
35: 4. When in doubt, choose the higher level
36: 5. Treat cross-phase coordination as a reason to escalate even when raw LOC stays modest
37: 
38: ---
39: 
40: ## 2. DECISION MATRIX TABLE (Progressive Enhancement)
41: 
42: | Level               | LOC Guidance     | Required Files                                                      | Adds To Previous                   | Use When                             |
43: | ------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
44: | **1: Baseline**     | <100             | `spec.md` + `plan.md` + `tasks.md` + `implementation-summary.md`    | (foundation)                       | All features - minimum documentation |
45: | **2: Verification** | 100-499          | Level 1 + `checklist.md`                                            | QA checklist                       | Needs systematic validation          |
46: | **3: Full**         | ≥500             | Level 2 + `decision-record.md`                                      | ADR + optional research            | Complex/architectural changes        |
47: | **3+: Extended**    | Complexity 80+   | Level 3 + approval workflow + compliance + stakeholders             | Review tracking + coordination     | High-complexity, multi-workstream    |
48: 
49: **Progressive Enhancement Model:**
50: ```text
51: Level 1 (Baseline):     spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
52:                               ↓
53: Level 2 (Verification): Level 1 + checklist.md
54:                               ↓
55: Level 3 (Full):         Level 2 + decision-record.md + optional research/research.md + optional resource-map.md (any level)
56:                               ↓
57: Level 3+ (Extended):    Level 3 + approval workflow + compliance + stakeholder tracking
58: ```
59: 
60: ---
61: 
62: ## 3. LOC AS SOFT GUIDANCE (Not Enforcement)
63: 
64: **How to count:**
65: - Count all files being modified
66: - Include new files being created
67: - Estimate conservatively (round up when uncertain)
68: 
69: **LOC thresholds suggest (not enforce):**
70: - **<100 LOC** → Suggests Level 1 (Baseline)
71: - **100-499 LOC** → Suggests Level 2 (Verification)
72: - **≥500 LOC** → Suggests Level 3 (Full)
73: 
74: **Key distinction:**
75: - LOC thresholds are **SOFT GUIDANCE** - use judgment
76: - **Enforcement is MANUAL** - verify required templates exist before claiming completion
77: - Risk/complexity can override LOC (e.g., 50 LOC security change = Level 2+)
78: 
79: **Note:** Single typo/whitespace fixes (<5 characters in one file) are exempt from spec requirements.
80: 
81: ---
82: 
83: ## 4. SECONDARY FACTORS (CAN OVERRIDE LOC)
84: 
85: These factors can push you to a higher level even if LOC suggests lower:
86: 
87: ### 1. Complexity
88: - **Simple refactor** (no new logic) → May stay at lower level
89: - **Architectural change** (new patterns) → Escalate to higher level
90: - **Example**: 200 LOC refactor might stay Level 2, but 200 LOC architectural change could be Level 3
91: 
92: ### 2. Risk
93: - **Config cascades** → Higher level (documentation protects against mistakes)
94: - **Authentication/security changes** → Higher level (security implications)
95: - **Example**: 50 LOC config change affecting multiple systems → Level 2 (risk trumps LOC)
96: 
97: ### 3. Dependencies
98: - **Single component** → Lower level acceptable
99: - **Multiple systems affected** → Higher level needed
100: - **Example**: 80 LOC touching 5 different modules → Level 2 (coordination needed)
101: 
102: ### 4. Testing Needs
103: - **Unit tests only** → Lower level acceptable
104: - **Integration/E2E tests required** → Higher level needed
105: - **Example**: 95 LOC requiring complex integration testing → Level 2 (testing strategy needed)
106: 
107: ---
108: 
109: ## 5. EDGE CASE GUIDANCE
110: 
111: | Scenario                | LOC | Suggested Level | Required Files                  | Rationale                                |
112: | ----------------------- | --- | --------------- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
113: | Typo in one file        | 1   | Exempt          | None                            | Truly trivial (<5 chars, single file)    |
114: | Typo across 5 files     | 5   | Level 1         | spec + plan + tasks + implementation-summary | Multi-file coordination                  |
115: | 95 LOC feature          | 95  | Level 1         | spec + plan + tasks + implementation-summary | Under threshold, baseline docs           |
116: | 105 LOC feature         | 105 | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Just over, needs QA validation           |
117: | Refactor (no new logic) | 200 | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Complexity needs verification            |
118: | Config cascade          | 50  | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Risk trumps LOC - needs validation       |
119: | Authentication change   | 80  | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Security implications require QA         |
120: | System redesign         | 300 | Level 3         | L2 + decision-record            | Architectural decisions must be recorded |
121: | Multi-team project      | 400 | Level 3         | L2 + decision-record + research | Coordination needs full documentation    |
122: 
123: ---
124: 
125: ## 6. WHEN IN DOUBT
126: 
127: **Choose the higher level.**
128: 
129: **Reasoning:**
130: - Better to over-document than under-document
131: - Higher level provides more structure and guidance
132: - Easier to skip optional sections than add missing documentation later
133: - Future you will thank present you for the extra context
134: 
135: ### Template Style Selection
136: 
137: In addition to level, choose the appropriate template **style**:
138: 
139: Templates are selected by `templates/manifest/spec-kit-docs.json` and rendered through `create.sh`.
140: 
141: ---
142: 
143: ## 7. CHECKLIST QUALITY REQUIREMENTS
144: 
145: Level 2+ documentation requires `checklist.md` with specific quality standards:
146: 
147: ### Priority Organization (P0/P1/P2)
148: 
149: All checklist items MUST be tagged with priority markers:
150: 
151: | Priority | Meaning       | Action Required                                   |
152: | -------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
153: | **P0**   | HARD BLOCKER  | Must complete before ANY other work               |
154: | **P1**   | Must complete | Required for completion OR user-approved deferral |
155: | **P2**   | Can defer     | Nice-to-have, can defer without approval          |
156: 
157: **Format Example:**
158: ```markdown
159: ## Implementation Checklist
160: 
161: ### P0 - Blockers
162: - [ ] Fix breaking test before proceeding
163: - [ ] Resolve merge conflict in main.js
164: 
165: ### P1 - Required
166: - [ ] Implement core validation logic
167: - [ ] Add unit tests for new functions
168: - [ ] Update documentation
169: 
170: ### P2 - Enhancements
171: - [ ] Add performance optimization
172: - [ ] Refactor legacy helper functions
173: ```
174: 
175: ### Evidence Citation Requirements
176: 
177: Completed checklist items MUST include verification evidence:
178: 
179: **Format:** `[x] Item description - [evidence type: details]`
180: 
181: **Evidence Types:**
182: - `[verified: grep output]` - Text search confirmation
183: - `[verified: test pass]` - Test suite confirmation
184: - `[verified: browser check]` - Manual browser verification
185: - `[verified: file exists]` - File presence confirmation
186: - `[verified: review]` - Code review confirmation
187: 
188: **Examples:**
189: ```markdown
190: - [x] Remove unused helper - [verified: grep shows 0 matches for 'helperName']
191: - [x] All tests pass - [verified: test pass - 47/47 tests green]
192: - [x] Modal displays correctly - [verified: browser check - Chrome/Firefox/Safari]
193: - [x] Config file created - [verified: file exists at config/settings.json]
194: ```
195: 
196: **Validation:** Completion claims are BLOCKED without evidence citations on P0/P1 items.
197: 
198: ---
199: 
200: ## 8. VALIDATION RULES REFERENCE
201: 
202: The spec validation system (`validate.sh`) checks documentation quality using these rules:
203: 
204: ### Rules by Applicability
205: 
206: | Rule                    | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3 | Severity | Description                                    |
207: | ----------------------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
208: | **FILE_EXISTS**         | ✓       | ✓       | ✓       | error    | Required files must exist for the level        |
209: | **PLACEHOLDER_FILLED**  | ✓       | ✓       | ✓       | warning  | Template placeholders must be replaced         |
210: | **ANCHORS_VALID**       | ✓       | ✓       | ✓       | warning  | Generated continuity artifacts must have balanced anchor pairs   |
211: | **CHECKLIST_HAS_ITEMS** | —       | ✓       | ✓       | warning  | checklist.md must contain actionable items     |
212: | **DECISION_RECORDED**   | —       | —       | ✓       | warning  | decision-record.md must document key decisions |
213: 
214: ### Rule Details
215: 
216: **FILE_EXISTS**
217: - Purpose: Ensures all required files for the documentation level are present
218: - Applies to: All levels (checks level-specific required files)
219: - Severity: error (blocks completion)
220: - Details: Level 1 requires `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `implementation-summary.md`; Level 2 adds `checklist.md`; Level 3 adds `decision-record.md`
221: 
222: **PLACEHOLDER_FILLED**
223: - Purpose: Detects unfilled template placeholders that indicate incomplete documentation
224: - Applies to: All levels
225: - Severity: warning (default)
226: - Details: Searches for patterns like `<PROJECT_NAME>`, `<DESCRIPTION>`, or other unresolved placeholder tokens.
227: 
228: **ANCHORS_VALID**
229: - Purpose: Validates that generated continuity artifacts have balanced ANCHOR open/close pairs
230: - Applies to: All levels (anchors in `implementation-summary.md` and other spec docs)
231: - Severity: warning (default)
232: - Details: Each `<!-- ANCHOR: id -->` must have matching `<!-- /ANCHOR: id -->`. Unbalanced anchors break semantic indexing of canonical spec docs.
233: 
234: **CHECKLIST_HAS_ITEMS**
235: - Purpose: Ensures checklist.md contains actual checklist items, not just template structure
236: - Applies to: Level 2+ only
237: - Severity: warning (default)
238: - Details: Looks for `- [ ]` or `- [x]` patterns indicating actionable items
239: 
240: **DECISION_RECORDED**
241: - Purpose: Verifies that architectural decisions are documented with rationale
242: - Applies to: Level 3 only
243: - Severity: warning (default)
244: - Details: Checks decision-record.md for decision entries with status and rationale
245: 
246: ### Severity Levels
247: 
248: | Severity    | Meaning                                    | Action Required            |
249: | ----------- | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------- |
250: | **error**   | Validation fails, blocks completion claims | Must fix before proceeding |
251: | **warning** | Issue detected, should address             | Address or acknowledge     |
252: | **info**    | Informational, no action required          | Optional improvement       |
253: 
254: ---
255: 
256: ## 9. LEVEL MIGRATION DURING IMPLEMENTATION
257: 
258: If scope grows during implementation, escalate by adding the required files:
259: 
260: | From Level | To Level                   | Action                                          | Files to Add                                                    |
261: | ---------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
262: | 1 → 2      | Add verification           | `checklist.md`                                  |                                                                 |
263: | 2 → 3      | Add decision documentation | `decision-record.md` (+ optional `research/research.md`) |                                                                 |
264: | 3 → 3+     | Add governance             | AI execution protocol + extended checklist      | Sign-off section in spec.md + multi-agent coordination patterns |
265: 
266: **Changelog example:**
267: ```markdown
268: ## Change Log
269: - [YYYY-MM-DD]: Created as Level 1 (simple bug fix) - spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md
270: - [YYYY-MM-DD]: Escalated to Level 2 (discovered validation needs) - added checklist.md
271: - [YYYY-MM-DD]: Escalated to Level 3 (architectural decision required) - added decision-record.md
272: ```
273: 
274: **Note:** Going down levels is rare (keep higher-level docs even if not all used).
275: 
276: ---
277: 
278: ## 10. QUICK DECISION FLOWCHART
279: 
280: ```text
281: Any file modification?
282:     ↓
283: Single typo? ──YES──→ Exempt (no spec needed)
284: (<5 chars, 1 file)
285:     │
286:     NO
287:     ↓
288: Start with Level 1 (Baseline)
289: Required: spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
290:     ↓
291: Needs QA validation? ──YES──→ Level 2 (add checklist.md)
292: (risk, multi-file, testing)
293:     │
294:     NO (stay Level 1)
295:     ↓
296: Architectural decision? ──YES──→ Level 3 (add decision-record.md)
297: (complex, arch impact)
298:     │
299:     NO (stay current level)
300: ```
301: 
302: **LOC as soft guidance (suggests level):**
303: - <100 LOC → Suggests Level 1
304: - 100-499 LOC → Suggests Level 2
305: - ≥500 LOC → Suggests Level 3
306: 
307: **Override factors (can bump level):**
308: - High complexity? → Consider higher level
309: - High risk? → Consider higher level
310: - Multiple dependencies? → Consider higher level
311: - Complex testing needs? → Consider higher level
312: 
313: **Final check:** If confidence < 80% on level choice → Ask user or choose higher level.
314: 
315: ---
316: 
317: ## 11. SPEC TRACEABILITY
318: 
319: This decision matrix addresses the following spec requirements:
320: 
321: | Requirement | Section | Description                                     |
322: | ----------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
323: | REQ-006     | §2.1    | Level 1 baseline documentation requirements     |
324: | REQ-007     | §2.2    | Level 2 verification requirements (checklist)   |
325: | REQ-008     | §2.3    | Level 3 full documentation (decision records)   |
326: | REQ-009     | §2.4    | Level 3+ extended documentation (governance)    |
327: | REQ-010     | §3      | LOC thresholds as soft guidance                 |
328: 
329: **Source**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md`
330: 
331: ---
332: 
333: ## 12. RELATED RESOURCES
334: 
335: ### Asset Files
336: - [complexity_decision_matrix.md](./complexity_decision_matrix.md) - Complexity-based level selection and scoring
337: - [parallel_dispatch_config.md](./parallel_dispatch_config.md) - Complexity scoring and agent dispatch
338: - [template_mapping.md](./template_mapping.md) - Template routing and task mapping
339: 
340: ### Reference Files
341: - level specifications reference - Complete Level 1-3+ requirements
342: - [quick_reference.md](../references/workflows/quick_reference.md) - Commands, checklists, and troubleshooting
343: - [template_guide.md](../references/templates/template_guide.md) - Template selection and quality standards
344: 
345: ### Scripts
346: - Level contract resolver - manifest-backed Level contract rendering
347: 
348: ### Related Skills
349: - `system-spec-kit` - Spec folder workflow orchestrator
350: - `sk-code` - Implementation, debugging, and verification lifecycle

(End of file - total 350 lines)
</content>

---
title: Level Decision Matrix
description: Quick reference for selecting the appropriate documentation level using progressive enhancement.
trigger_phrases:
  - "level decision matrix"
  - "documentation level selection"

---

# Level Decision Matrix - Complexity Scoring Guide

Quick reference for selecting documentation levels (1-3+) based on LOC guidance and complexity factors.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

This matrix helps determine the appropriate documentation level for any task. Use it when:
- Starting a new spec folder and need to choose Level 1, 2, or 3
- Scope changes mid-implementation and level escalation may be needed
- Uncertain whether complexity/risk factors should override LOC guidance
- Coordinating a campaign that spans multiple phase folders or runtime families

### Usage

1. Check LOC estimate against thresholds (<100, 100-499, ≥500)
2. Evaluate secondary factors (complexity, risk, dependencies, testing needs)
3. If factors suggest higher level than LOC indicates, escalate
4. When in doubt, choose the higher level
5. Treat cross-phase coordination as a reason to escalate even when raw LOC stays modest

---

## 2. DECISION MATRIX TABLE (Progressive Enhancement)

| Level               | LOC Guidance     | Required Files                                                      | Adds To Previous                   | Use When                             |
| ------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| **1: Baseline**     | <100             | `spec.md` + `plan.md` + `tasks.md` + `implementation-summary.md`    | (foundation)                       | All features - minimum documentation |
| **2: Verification** | 100-499          | Level 1 + `checklist.md`                                            | QA checklist                       | Needs systematic validation          |
| **3: Full**         | ≥500             | Level 2 + `decision-record.md`                                      | ADR + optional research            | Complex/architectural changes        |
| **3+: Extended**    | Complexity 80+   | Level 3 + approval workflow + compliance + stakeholders             | Review tracking + coordination     | High-complexity, multi-workstream    |

**Progressive Enhancement Model:**
```text
Level 1 (Baseline):     spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
                              ↓
Level 2 (Verification): Level 1 + checklist.md
                              ↓
Level 3 (Full):         Level 2 + decision-record.md + optional research/research.md + optional resource-map.md (any level)
                              ↓
Level 3+ (Extended):    Level 3 + approval workflow + compliance + stakeholder tracking
```

---

## 3. LOC AS SOFT GUIDANCE (Not Enforcement)

**How to count:**
- Count all files being modified
- Include new files being created
- Estimate conservatively (round up when uncertain)

**LOC thresholds suggest (not enforce):**
- **<100 LOC** → Suggests Level 1 (Baseline)
- **100-499 LOC** → Suggests Level 2 (Verification)
- **≥500 LOC** → Suggests Level 3 (Full)

**Key distinction:**
- LOC thresholds are **SOFT GUIDANCE** - use judgment
- **Enforcement is MANUAL** - verify required templates exist before claiming completion
- Risk/complexity can override LOC (e.g., 50 LOC security change = Level 2+)

**Note:** Single typo/whitespace fixes (<5 characters in one file) are exempt from spec requirements.

---

## 4. SECONDARY FACTORS (CAN OVERRIDE LOC)

These factors can push you to a higher level even if LOC suggests lower:

### 1. Complexity
- **Simple refactor** (no new logic) → May stay at lower level
- **Architectural change** (new patterns) → Escalate to higher level
- **Example**: 200 LOC refactor might stay Level 2, but 200 LOC architectural change could be Level 3

### 2. Risk
- **Config cascades** → Higher level (documentation protects against mistakes)
- **Authentication/security changes** → Higher level (security implications)
- **Example**: 50 LOC config change affecting multiple systems → Level 2 (risk trumps LOC)

### 3. Dependencies
- **Single component** → Lower level acceptable
- **Multiple systems affected** → Higher level needed
- **Example**: 80 LOC touching 5 different modules → Level 2 (coordination needed)

### 4. Testing Needs
- **Unit tests only** → Lower level acceptable
- **Integration/E2E tests required** → Higher level needed
- **Example**: 95 LOC requiring complex integration testing → Level 2 (testing strategy needed)

---

## 5. EDGE CASE GUIDANCE

| Scenario                | LOC | Suggested Level | Required Files                  | Rationale                                |
| ----------------------- | --- | --------------- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Typo in one file        | 1   | Exempt          | None                            | Truly trivial (<5 chars, single file)    |
| Typo across 5 files     | 5   | Level 1         | spec + plan + tasks + implementation-summary | Multi-file coordination                  |
| 95 LOC feature          | 95  | Level 1         | spec + plan + tasks + implementation-summary | Under threshold, baseline docs           |
| 105 LOC feature         | 105 | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Just over, needs QA validation           |
| Refactor (no new logic) | 200 | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Complexity needs verification            |
| Config cascade          | 50  | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Risk trumps LOC - needs validation       |
| Authentication change   | 80  | Level 2         | L1 + checklist                  | Security implications require QA         |
| System redesign         | 300 | Level 3         | L2 + decision-record            | Architectural decisions must be recorded |
| Multi-team project      | 400 | Level 3         | L2 + decision-record + research | Coordination needs full documentation    |

---

## 6. WHEN IN DOUBT

**Choose the higher level.**

**Reasoning:**
- Better to over-document than under-document
- Higher level provides more structure and guidance
- Easier to skip optional sections than add missing documentation later
- Future you will thank present you for the extra context

### Template Style Selection

In addition to level, choose the appropriate template **style**:

Templates are selected by `templates/manifest/spec-kit-docs.json` and rendered through `create.sh`.

---

## 7. CHECKLIST QUALITY REQUIREMENTS

Level 2+ documentation requires `checklist.md` with specific quality standards:

### Priority Organization (P0/P1/P2)

All checklist items MUST be tagged with priority markers:

| Priority | Meaning       | Action Required                                   |
| -------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| **P0**   | HARD BLOCKER  | Must complete before ANY other work               |
| **P1**   | Must complete | Required for completion OR user-approved deferral |
| **P2**   | Can defer     | Nice-to-have, can defer without approval          |

**Format Example:**
```markdown
## Implementation Checklist

### P0 - Blockers
- [ ] Fix breaking test before proceeding
- [ ] Resolve merge conflict in main.js

### P1 - Required
- [ ] Implement core validation logic
- [ ] Add unit tests for new functions
- [ ] Update documentation

### P2 - Enhancements
- [ ] Add performance optimization
- [ ] Refactor legacy helper functions
```

### Evidence Citation Requirements

Completed checklist items MUST include verification evidence:

**Format:** `[x] Item description - [evidence type: details]`

**Evidence Types:**
- `[verified: grep output]` - Text search confirmation
- `[verified: test pass]` - Test suite confirmation
- `[verified: browser check]` - Manual browser verification
- `[verified: file exists]` - File presence confirmation
- `[verified: review]` - Code review confirmation

**Examples:**
```markdown
- [x] Remove unused helper - [verified: grep shows 0 matches for 'helperName']
- [x] All tests pass - [verified: test pass - 47/47 tests green]
- [x] Modal displays correctly - [verified: browser check - Chrome/Firefox/Safari]
- [x] Config file created - [verified: file exists at config/settings.json]
```

**Validation:** Completion claims are BLOCKED without evidence citations on P0/P1 items.

---

## 8. VALIDATION RULES REFERENCE

The spec validation system (`validate.sh`) checks documentation quality using these rules:

### Rules by Applicability

| Rule                    | Level 1 | Level 2 | Level 3 | Severity | Description                                    |
| ----------------------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| **FILE_EXISTS**         | ✓       | ✓       | ✓       | error    | Required files must exist for the level        |
| **PLACEHOLDER_FILLED**  | ✓       | ✓       | ✓       | warning  | Template placeholders must be replaced         |
| **ANCHORS_VALID**       | ✓       | ✓       | ✓       | warning  | Generated continuity artifacts must have balanced anchor pairs   |
| **CHECKLIST_HAS_ITEMS** | —       | ✓       | ✓       | warning  | checklist.md must contain actionable items     |
| **DECISION_RECORDED**   | —       | —       | ✓       | warning  | decision-record.md must document key decisions |

### Rule Details

**FILE_EXISTS**
- Purpose: Ensures all required files for the documentation level are present
- Applies to: All levels (checks level-specific required files)
- Severity: error (blocks completion)
- Details: Level 1 requires `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `implementation-summary.md`; Level 2 adds `checklist.md`; Level 3 adds `decision-record.md`

**PLACEHOLDER_FILLED**
- Purpose: Detects unfilled template placeholders that indicate incomplete documentation
- Applies to: All levels
- Severity: warning (default)
- Details: Searches for patterns like `<PROJECT_NAME>`, `<DESCRIPTION>`, or other unresolved placeholder tokens.

**ANCHORS_VALID**
- Purpose: Validates that generated continuity artifacts have balanced ANCHOR open/close pairs
- Applies to: All levels (anchors in `implementation-summary.md` and other spec docs)
- Severity: warning (default)
- Details: Each `<!-- ANCHOR: id -->` must have matching `<!-- /ANCHOR: id -->`. Unbalanced anchors break semantic indexing of canonical spec docs.

**CHECKLIST_HAS_ITEMS**
- Purpose: Ensures checklist.md contains actual checklist items, not just template structure
- Applies to: Level 2+ only
- Severity: warning (default)
- Details: Looks for `- [ ]` or `- [x]` patterns indicating actionable items

**DECISION_RECORDED**
- Purpose: Verifies that architectural decisions are documented with rationale
- Applies to: Level 3 only
- Severity: warning (default)
- Details: Checks decision-record.md for decision entries with status and rationale

### Severity Levels

| Severity    | Meaning                                    | Action Required            |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------- |
| **error**   | Validation fails, blocks completion claims | Must fix before proceeding |
| **warning** | Issue detected, should address             | Address or acknowledge     |
| **info**    | Informational, no action required          | Optional improvement       |

---

## 9. LEVEL MIGRATION DURING IMPLEMENTATION

If scope grows during implementation, escalate by adding the required files:

| From Level | To Level                   | Action                                          | Files to Add                                                    |
| ---------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 → 2      | Add verification           | `checklist.md`                                  |                                                                 |
| 2 → 3      | Add decision documentation | `decision-record.md` (+ optional `research/research.md`) |                                                                 |
| 3 → 3+     | Add governance             | AI execution protocol + extended checklist      | Sign-off section in spec.md + multi-agent coordination patterns |

**Changelog example:**
```markdown
## Change Log
- [YYYY-MM-DD]: Created as Level 1 (simple bug fix) - spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md
- [YYYY-MM-DD]: Escalated to Level 2 (discovered validation needs) - added checklist.md
- [YYYY-MM-DD]: Escalated to Level 3 (architectural decision required) - added decision-record.md
```

**Note:** Going down levels is rare (keep higher-level docs even if not all used).

---

## 10. QUICK DECISION FLOWCHART

```text
Any file modification?
    ↓
Single typo? ──YES──→ Exempt (no spec needed)
(<5 chars, 1 file)
    │
    NO
    ↓
Start with Level 1 (Baseline)
Required: spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md + implementation-summary.md
    ↓
Needs QA validation? ──YES──→ Level 2 (add checklist.md)
(risk, multi-file, testing)
    │
    NO (stay Level 1)
    ↓
Architectural decision? ──YES──→ Level 3 (add decision-record.md)
(complex, arch impact)
    │
    NO (stay current level)
```

**LOC as soft guidance (suggests level):**
- <100 LOC → Suggests Level 1
- 100-499 LOC → Suggests Level 2
- ≥500 LOC → Suggests Level 3

**Override factors (can bump level):**
- High complexity? → Consider higher level
- High risk? → Consider higher level
- Multiple dependencies? → Consider higher level
- Complex testing needs? → Consider higher level

**Final check:** If confidence < 80% on level choice → Ask user or choose higher level.

---

## 11. SPEC TRACEABILITY

This decision matrix addresses the following spec requirements:

| Requirement | Section | Description                                     |
| ----------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| REQ-006     | §2.1    | Level 1 baseline documentation requirements     |
| REQ-007     | §2.2    | Level 2 verification requirements (checklist)   |
| REQ-008     | §2.3    | Level 3 full documentation (decision records)   |
| REQ-009     | §2.4    | Level 3+ extended documentation (governance)    |
| REQ-010     | §3      | LOC thresholds as soft guidance                 |

**Source**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md`

---

## 12. RELATED RESOURCES

### Asset Files
- [complexity_decision_matrix.md](./complexity_decision_matrix.md) - Complexity-based level selection and scoring
- [parallel_dispatch_config.md](./parallel_dispatch_config.md) - Complexity scoring and agent dispatch
- [template_mapping.md](./template_mapping.md) - Template routing and task mapping

### Reference Files
- level specifications reference - Complete Level 1-3+ requirements
- [quick_reference.md](../references/workflows/quick_reference.md) - Commands, checklists, and troubleshooting
- [template_guide.md](../references/templates/template_guide.md) - Template selection and quality standards

### Scripts
- Level contract resolver - manifest-backed Level contract rendering

### Related Skills
- `system-spec-kit` - Spec folder workflow orchestrator
- `sk-code` - Implementation, debugging, and verification lifecycle

18: # Spec-Folder Naming & Rename Convention
19: 
20: ## Rule
21: 
22: Spec folders use `NNN-short-descriptive-name`. Canonical source: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/folder_structure.md` §2.
23: 
24: ## Name format
25: 
26: - Number prefix: **3 digits, zero-padded** (`007`, not `7`).
27: - Single hyphen separator; name lowercase, hyphen-separated, short + descriptive (2-4 words).
28: - Describe the work/outcome literally, NOT the method or tooling.
29: - Good: `001-initial-setup`, `042-refactor-api-endpoints`. Bad: `1-setup` (no zero-pad), `001_setup` (underscore), `001-Setup` (uppercase).
30: - Child/sub-folders use the same `NNN-topic-name` form, numbered sequentially per parent. Next child = highest existing child number + 1 (gaps are NOT auto-backfilled).
31: - Slug rule: lowercase; every non-`[a-z0-9]` → `-`; collapse repeats; trim leading/trailing `-`.
32: 
33: ## Renaming or moving (per `references/workflows/rename_pattern.md`)
34:

37: 3. REGENERATE compiled indexes, never hand-edit (e.g. advisor `skill-graph.json` via `skill_graph_compiler.py --export-json`).
38: 4. Reconcile parent metadata via `generate-context.js` (refreshes `children_ids` + `derived.last_active_child_id`).
39: 5. Stay on `main`. Final gate: `validate.sh --strict <spec-folder>` exit 0.

# Spec-Folder Naming & Rename Convention

## Rule

Spec folders use `NNN-short-descriptive-name`. Canonical source: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/structure/folder_structure.md` §2.

## Name format

- Number prefix: **3 digits, zero-padded** (`007`, not `7`).
- Single hyphen separator; name lowercase, hyphen-separated, short + descriptive (2-4 words).
- Describe the work/outcome literally, NOT the method or tooling.
- Good: `001-initial-setup`, `042-refactor-api-endpoints`. Bad: `1-setup` (no zero-pad), `001_setup` (underscore), `001-Setup` (uppercase).
- Child/sub-folders use the same `NNN-topic-name` form, numbered sequentially per parent. Next child = highest existing child number + 1 (gaps are NOT auto-backfilled).
- Slug rule: lowercase; every non-`[a-z0-9]` → `-`; collapse repeats; trim leading/trailing `-`.

## Renaming or moving (per `references/workflows/rename_pattern.md`)

1. `git mv` the folder (preserves `git log --follow`). NEVER delete + recreate.
2. Update LIVE cross-references (parent `graph-metadata.json`, `description.json`, command files, sibling links) by literal substitution. PRESERVE historical narrative verbatim.
3. REGENERATE compiled indexes, never hand-edit (e.g. advisor `skill-graph.json` via `skill_graph_compiler.py --export-json`).
4. Reconcile parent metadata via `generate-context.js` (refreshes `children_ids` + `derived.last_active_child_id`).
5. Stay on `main`. Final gate: `validate.sh --strict <spec-folder>` exit 0.

# GATE ENFORCEMENT - Edge Cases & Cross-Reference

> Lean constitutional memory. Full gate definitions live in **AGENTS.md Section 2**.
> This file adds: compaction recovery, continuation validation, and trigger-based surfacing.

## Gate Cross-Reference

Full definitions: **AGENTS.md § 2 — MANDATORY GATES**

| Gate       | Type           | One-Line Summary                                                          |
| ---------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Gate 1** | SOFT           | Understanding + context surfacing on each user message                    |
| **Gate 2** | REQUIRED       | Skill routing via Skill Advisor Hook (primary) or the `system-skill-advisor` Python compat shim |
| **Gate 3** | **HARD BLOCK** | Spec folder A/B/C/D/E question before ANY file modification               |
| **Gate 4** | **HARD BLOCK** | Skill-owned workflow enforcement for iterative loops (`:auto` suffix etc) |

**Critical:** Gate 3 overrides Gates 1-2. If file modification detected → ask spec folder FIRST, analyze after.
**Critical:** Gate 4 enforces canonical command surfaces for deep-research/deep-review loops; cannot be bypassed via direct agent dispatch or `/tmp` state.

## Compaction Recovery

**TRIGGER:** Context compaction event (user mentions "compaction", "context lost", explicit `/compact` invocation, or post-compaction system reminder).

**ACTION:**
1. STOP — do not take any action, do not use any tools.
2. Re-read the project AGENTS.md (canonical) and any active spec folder's `handover.md` / `_memory.continuity` / `implementation-summary.md` ladder.
3. Summarise:
   - Current task and status
   - User's most recent instruction (especially constraints like "plan only", "discuss first")
   - Modified files and their current state
   - Any errors encountered and their resolutions
   - Current git branch and uncommitted changes
4. Present this summary and WAIT for confirmation before proceeding.
5. Do NOT assume the compaction summary's implied next steps are correct.

**Always preserve through compaction:**
- Exact file paths of all modifications
- Error messages verbatim
- The user's last 2-3 instructions with exact wording
- Any "do not" or "avoid" constraints from the user

After 2+ compactions in one session, recommend `/clear` and a fresh start.

## Continuation Validation

**TRIGGER:** User message contains "CONTINUATION - Attempt" pattern.

**ACTION:**
1. Parse handoff for: Spec folder path, Last Action, Next Action
2. Validate against canonical packet continuity in this order: `handover.md` -> `_memory.continuity` -> spec docs
3. IF mismatch → Report and ask:
   - A) Trust handoff message
   - B) Trust canonical continuity
   - C) Investigate both
4. IF validated → Proceed with "Continuation validated"

**RATIONALE:** Handoff messages may be stale or from a different branch. Always cross-check against the packet's canonical continuity surfaces before continuing.

## Quick Reference

| Rule                    | Trigger                                | Source            |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| Gates 1-4               | See above                              | AGENTS.md § 2     |
| Memory Save Rule        | "save context/memory"                  | AGENTS.md § 2     |
| Completion Verification | "done/complete/finished"               | AGENTS.md § 2     |
| Violation Recovery      | About to skip gates / gates skipped    | AGENTS.md § 2     |
| Compaction Recovery     | Context loss / compaction              | **This file**     |
| Continuation Validation | "CONTINUATION - Attempt"               | **This file**     |

*Constitutional Memory — Always surfaces at top of search results*

14: # Spec Folder Authoring Checklist
15: 
16: Checklist for writing spec folders that satisfy the Level contract, continuity frontmatter, metadata files, and strict validation.
17: 
18: ## 1. OVERVIEW
19: 
20: ### Purpose
21: 
22: This checklist keeps spec folder writes compatible with the system-spec-kit validator and memory resume ladder. It is the companion checklist to the spec folder write recipe and focuses on required files, anchors, metadata, and strict validation.
23: 
24: ### Usage
25: 
26: - Use this when authoring a new spec folder under `specs/` or `.opencode/specs/`.
27: - Use this when modifying a spec folder's canonical docs, frontmatter, `_memory.continuity`, or metadata files.
28: - Use this when creating a phase child under a phase parent.
29: - Use this when a workflow writes `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, `description.json`, or `graph-metadata.json`.
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. PRE-CHECKS
34: 
35: - [ ] Read the Level 1 contract files in an existing packet such as `<spec-folder>`.
36: - [ ] Read templates from `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/` and the manifest entries under `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/`.
37: - [ ] Verify the required Level 1 docs: `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, and `implementation-summary.md`.
38: - [ ] Confirm required anchor counts for the selected level before writing.
39: - [ ] Confirm `_memory.continuity.packet_pointer` is slash-separated and relative to the specs track.
40: - [ ] Confirm `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` will be refreshed after authoring.
41: 
42: ---
43: 
44: ## 3. STEPS
45: 
46: 1. Decide the spec root, packet number, short name, phase child name, and documentation level.
47: 2. Scaffold from system-spec-kit templates or `create.sh` instead of freehanding the contract.
48: 3. Author `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, and `implementation-summary.md` with canonical anchors.
49: 4. Add `_memory.continuity` frontmatter with compact `recent_action`, ISO-8601 UTC `last_updated_at`, and a full relative `packet_pointer`.
50: 5. Refresh `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` with the system-spec-kit metadata scripts.
51: 6. Run strict validation and fix every warning as a failure.
52: 7. Record verification evidence in the implementation summary or handoff output.
53: 
54: ---
55: 
56: ## 4. POST-CHECKS
57: 
58: - [ ] Run `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh <path> --strict`.
59: - [ ] grep verification: `rg -n "ANCHOR:metadata|ANCHOR:summary|ANCHOR:notation|_memory:|packet_pointer" <path>`.

61: - [ ] Cross-runtime mirror parity check if the spec folder changes command or agent mirrors.
62: - [ ] Confirm the companion recipe was followed: `spec_folder_write_recipe.md`.

64: ---

66: ## 5. RELATED RESOURCES

# Spec Folder Authoring Checklist

Checklist for writing spec folders that satisfy the Level contract, continuity frontmatter, metadata files, and strict validation.

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

This checklist keeps spec folder writes compatible with the system-spec-kit validator and memory resume ladder. It is the companion checklist to the spec folder write recipe and focuses on required files, anchors, metadata, and strict validation.

### Usage

- Use this when authoring a new spec folder under `specs/` or `.opencode/specs/`.
- Use this when modifying a spec folder's canonical docs, frontmatter, `_memory.continuity`, or metadata files.
- Use this when creating a phase child under a phase parent.
- Use this when a workflow writes `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, `description.json`, or `graph-metadata.json`.

---

## 2. PRE-CHECKS

- [ ] Read the Level 1 contract files in an existing packet such as `<spec-folder>`.
- [ ] Read templates from `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/` and the manifest entries under `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/`.
- [ ] Verify the required Level 1 docs: `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, and `implementation-summary.md`.
- [ ] Confirm required anchor counts for the selected level before writing.
- [ ] Confirm `_memory.continuity.packet_pointer` is slash-separated and relative to the specs track.
- [ ] Confirm `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` will be refreshed after authoring.

---

## 3. STEPS

1. Decide the spec root, packet number, short name, phase child name, and documentation level.
2. Scaffold from system-spec-kit templates or `create.sh` instead of freehanding the contract.
3. Author `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, and `implementation-summary.md` with canonical anchors.
4. Add `_memory.continuity` frontmatter with compact `recent_action`, ISO-8601 UTC `last_updated_at`, and a full relative `packet_pointer`.
5. Refresh `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` with the system-spec-kit metadata scripts.
6. Run strict validation and fix every warning as a failure.
7. Record verification evidence in the implementation summary or handoff output.

---

## 4. POST-CHECKS

- [ ] Run `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh <path> --strict`.
- [ ] grep verification: `rg -n "ANCHOR:metadata|ANCHOR:summary|ANCHOR:notation|_memory:|packet_pointer" <path>`.
- [ ] grep verification: `rg -n "\"title\"|\"contextType\"|\"derived\"" <path>/description.json <path>/graph-metadata.json`.
- [ ] Cross-runtime mirror parity check if the spec folder changes command or agent mirrors.
- [ ] Confirm the companion recipe was followed: `spec_folder_write_recipe.md`.

---

## 5. RELATED RESOURCES

- sk-doc templates and system-spec-kit/templates/manifest/ (source-of-truth for document contract rules)
- Prior example: `<spec-folder>`
- Verification recipes: `spec_folder_write_recipe.md`

14: # Folder Structure Reference
15: 
16: Spec folder naming conventions, level requirements, and organization patterns.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: This document covers spec folder organization, naming conventions, and level-specific requirements for the Spec Kit system.
23: 
24: ### Template Directory Structure
25: 
26: ```
27: templates/
28: ├── manifest/                       # Canonical .tmpl source files + Level contract
29: │   ├── spec.md.tmpl
30: │   ├── plan.md.tmpl
31: │   ├── tasks.md.tmpl
32: │   ├── checklist.md.tmpl
33: │   ├── decision-record.md.tmpl
34: │   ├── implementation-summary.md.tmpl
35: │   ├── phase-parent.spec.md.tmpl
36: │   ├── handover.md.tmpl
37: │   ├── debug-delegation.md.tmpl
38: │   ├── research.md.tmpl
39: │   ├── resource-map.md.tmpl
40: │   ├── context-index.md.tmpl
41: │   └── spec-kit-docs.json          # Level contract resolved by create.sh
42: │
43: ├── examples/               # Rendered examples by Level
44: ├── changelog/              # Template history
45: ├── stress_test/            # Review/research rubric assets
46: └── scratch/                # Local ignored render workspace
47: ```
48: 
49: > **IMPORTANT:** Always scaffold new specs from the manifest-backed Level contract. `create.sh` and the Level contract resolver share `templates/manifest/spec-kit-docs.json`.
50: 
51: ---
52: 
53: ## 2. NAMING CONVENTION
54: 
55: ### Spec Folder Names
56: 
57: Format: `NNN-short-descriptive-name`
58: 
59: | Component | Rule | Example |
60: |-----------|------|---------|
61: | Number prefix | 3 digits, zero-padded | `007` |
62: | Separator | Single hyphen | `-` |
63: | Name | Lowercase, hyphen-separated | `add-auth-system` |
64: 
65: **Examples:**
66: - ✅ `001-initial-setup`
67: - ✅ `042-refactor-api-endpoints`
68: - ❌ `1-setup` (missing zero-padding)
69: - ❌ `001_setup` (underscore instead of hyphen)
70: - ❌ `001-Setup` (uppercase)
71: 
72: ### Sub-Folder Names
73: 
74: For iterative work within a spec folder:
75: 
76: Format: `NNN-topic-name`
77: 
78: ```
79: 007-feature/
80: ├── 001-initial-implementation/
81: ├── 002-bug-fixes/
82: └── 003-performance-optimization/
83: ```
84: 
85: ---
86: 
87: ## 3. LEVEL REQUIREMENTS
88: 
89: ### Level 1 (< 100 LOC)
90: 
91: **Required Files:**
92: ```
93: specs/NNN-name/
94: ├── spec.md                    # Problem statement, goals, scope
95: ├── plan.md                    # Implementation approach
96: ├── tasks.md                   # Task breakdown
97: └── implementation-summary.md  # Created AFTER implementation completes
98: ```
99: 
100: **Optional:**
101: - `scratch/` - Temporary files
102: - `memory/` - Retired compatibility folder for older packets only; current continuity lives in packet docs plus `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json`
103: 
104: ### Level 2 (100-499 LOC)
105: 
106: **Required Files:**
107: ```
108: specs/NNN-name/
109: ├── spec.md
110: ├── plan.md
111: ├── tasks.md
112: ├── checklist.md               # QA validation items
113: └── implementation-summary.md  # Created AFTER implementation completes
114: ```
115: 
116: **Optional:**
117: - `scratch/`
118: - `memory/` - Retired compatibility folder for older packets only; current save workflows skip new `[spec]/memory/*.md` writes
119: 
120: ### Level 3 (≥ 500 LOC)
121: 
122: **Required Files:**
123: ```
124: specs/NNN-name/
125: ├── spec.md
126: ├── plan.md
127: ├── tasks.md
128: ├── checklist.md
129: ├── decision-record.md         # Architecture decisions
130: └── implementation-summary.md  # Created AFTER implementation completes
131: ```
132: 
133: **Optional:**
134: - `scratch/`
135: - `memory/` - Retired compatibility folder for older packets only; current save workflows skip new `[spec]/memory/*.md` writes
136: - `research/` / `review/` local-owner folders — see §4 `research/` and `review/` (local owner folders). Root specs keep them at the root packet; child phases and sub-phases keep them under the owning phase folder.
137: 
138: ---
139: 
140: ## 4. SPECIAL FOLDERS
141: 
142: ### scratch/
143: 
144: Temporary, disposable files. Cleaned up after task completion.
145: 
146: **Use for:**
147: - Debug logs
148: - Test scripts
149: - Prototypes
150: - Temporary data
151: 
152: **Rules:**
153: - Never commit sensitive data
154: - Clean up when done
155: - Don't reference from permanent docs
156: 
157: ### memory/
158: 
159: Retired compatibility folder for older packets. Current save workflows skip legacy `[spec]/memory/*.md` writes; canonical continuity lives in packet docs (`handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, and related spec documents) plus `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` metadata.
160: 
161: **Use for:**
162: - Reading or migrating historical packets that already contain legacy memory artifacts
163: - Compatibility with old links that still point at `[spec]/memory/*.md`
164: 
165: **Current save path:** do not create new `memory/` session files; use `/memory:save` so continuity routes into canonical packet docs and metadata.
166: 
167: **Legacy File Naming:** `DD-MM-YY_HH-MM__topic-name.md`
168: 
169: Example: `07-12-25_14-30__feature-name.md`
170: 
171: ```markdown
172: ---
173: title: Session Summary
174: created: DD-MM-YY
175: type: context
176: triggers:
177:   - keyword1
178:   - keyword2
179: ---
180: 
181: # Content here
182: ```
183: 
184: ### research/ and review/ (local owner folders)
185: 
186: Deep-research and deep-review artifacts (iterations, deltas, prompts, state logs, synthesis) live under the **target spec folder's local** `research/` or `review/` folder. Root specs, child phases, and sub-phases all use a flat-first layout at `{spec_folder}/research/` or `{spec_folder}/review/` for first runs. A `pt-NN` packet directory is allocated only when prior local content already exists for a different target.
187: 
188: **Why:** the owning phase keeps its own deep-loop artifacts local, nested runs do not spill into ancestor roots, and resume/restart logic can stay bound to the exact target spec instead of re-resolving through a coordination parent.
189: 
190: **Layout (root spec plus flat-first child phases):**
191: 
192: ```text
193: <spec-folder>
194: ├── spec.md
195: ├── research/                               <- root-spec deep-research artifacts
196: ├── review/                                 <- root-spec deep-review artifacts
197: ├── 019-system-hardening/
198: │   ├── spec.md
199: │   ├── research/                               <- first run is flat
200: │   │   ├── deep-research-config.json
201: │   │   ├── deep-research-state.jsonl
202: │   │   ├── deep-research-strategy.md
203: │   │   ├── deep-research-dashboard.md
204: │   │   ├── findings-registry.json
205: │   │   ├── research.md
206: │   │   ├── iterations/iteration-NNN.md
207: │   │   ├── deltas/iter-NNN.jsonl
208: │   │   └── prompts/iteration-N.md
209: │   └── review/                                 <- first run is flat
210: └── 020-skill-advisor-hook-surface/
211:     ├── spec.md
212:     ├── research/
213:     │   └── 020-skill-advisor-hook-surface-pt-01/ <- conditional: prior non-matching content already existed
214:     └── review/
215: ```
216: 
217: **Naming (when a packet subfolder is allocated):** `{ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}/`
218: - `ownerSlug` = the owning spec folder name by default (for example `019-system-hardening` or `003-gate-c-writer-ready`)
219: - `NN` = two-digit zero-padded sequential counter per owner folder when a new packet must be allocated
220: 
221: **Flat-first convention (post-028):** child-phase first runs go directly under `{spec_folder}/research/` or `{spec_folder}/review/` with no `-pt-NN` subfolder. A packet subfolder is allocated only when prior content already exists for a non-matching target. Continuation runs reuse the existing flat artifact (or matching `pt-NN` packet). Root specs always use the flat path.
222: 
223: **Required resolver:** always use `resolveArtifactRoot(specFolder, 'research' | 'review')` from [`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs`](../../shared/review-research-paths.cjs). It resolves the local owner folder, returns flat for first runs and matching continuations, reuses an existing packet for the same target when present, and allocates a `pt-NN` packet only when prior content for a non-matching target exists. Never hand-pick the path.
224: 
225: **Forbidden:** creating or continuing child-phase research/review packets under an ancestor/root spec's `research/` or `review/` folder.
226: 
227: **See also:** `system-deep-loop/deep-research/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`, `system-deep-loop/deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md`, and the `step_resolve_artifact_root` block in `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_research_auto.yaml`.
228: 
229: ---
230: 
231: ## 5. ARCHIVE PATTERN
232: 
233: Completed or superseded specs use the `z_archive/` prefix:
234: 
235: ```
236: specs/
237: ├── 001-active-feature/
238: ├── 002-in-progress/
239: └── z_archive/
240:     ├── 001-completed-feature/
241:     └── 002-abandoned-approach/
242: ```
243: 
244: **Archive Triggers:**
245: - Feature fully implemented and verified
246: - Approach abandoned for alternative
247: - Spec superseded by newer version
248: 
249: ---
250: 
251: ## 6. EXAMPLE STRUCTURES
252: 
253: ### Simple Feature (Level 1)
254: 
255: ```
256: specs/015-add-dark-mode/
257: ├── spec.md
258: ├── plan.md
259: ├── tasks.md
260: └── implementation-summary.md  # Created after implementation
261: ```
262: 
263: ### Medium Feature (Level 2)
264: 
265: ```
266: specs/016-user-preferences/
267: ├── spec.md
268: ├── plan.md
269: ├── tasks.md
270: ├── checklist.md
271: ├── implementation-summary.md  # Created after implementation
272: ├── scratch/
273: │   └── test-data.json
274: └── memory/
275:     └── 15-01-24_10-30__user-preferences.md
276: ```
277: 
278: ### Complex Feature (Level 3)
279: 
280: ```
281: specs/017-authentication-system/
282: ├── spec.md
283: ├── plan.md
284: ├── tasks.md
285: ├── checklist.md
286: ├── decision-record.md
287: ├── implementation-summary.md
288: ├── scratch/
289: │   ├── oauth-flow-test.js
290: │   └── token-debug.log
291: └── memory/
292:     ├── 10-01-24_09-15__authentication-system.md
293:     └── 12-01-24_14-00__authentication-system.md
294: ```
295: 
296: ### Iterative Work (Sub-folders)
297: 
298: ```
299: specs/018-api-refactor/
300: ├── 001-endpoint-analysis/
301: │   ├── spec.md
302: │   ├── plan.md
303: │   └── tasks.md
304: ├── 002-breaking-changes/
305: │   ├── spec.md
306: │   ├── plan.md
307: │   ├── tasks.md
308: │   └── checklist.md
309: └── 003-migration-guide/
310:     ├── spec.md
311:     └── plan.md
312: ```
313: 
314: ```
315: specs/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/        <- Coordination root (point-in-time snapshot)
316: ├── spec.md                          <- Phase map + current tree truth
317: ├── decision-record.md               <- ADR-001: tree truth > historical synthesis
318: ├── 001-epic/                        <- Child phase packet
319: ├── 002-indexing/                    <- Child phase packet
320: └── 011-skill-alignment/             <- Child phase packet
321:     └── 002-skill-review/            <- Nested child
322: ```
323: 
324: ---
325: 
326: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
327: 
328: - Level specifications reference
329: - [Template Guide](../templates/template_guide.md)
330: - [Sub-folder Versioning](../structure/sub_folder_versioning.md)

(End of file - total 330 lines)
</content>
